title: Living in Boulder Vlog 4 - 2 - 2017 Still doing Area Dimensions

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on April 3, 2017, giving a casual update on his day: working on his website building downloadable tables for race distances and circles, troubleshooting mobile display issues, trying to monetize his site (only $230 total earned), going to lunch with his mom, and planning to study libertarian content online to rebut it.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Frustrated that his ad-heavy site earns almost nothing despite heavy work; skeptical of claims that two million people make money from websites
Views them as enemies funded by rich billionaires to spread 'mind-fucking' propaganda; suspects prominent libertarian voices are paid shills
Acknowledges his site looks broken on mobile and needs to fix it, but is deferring it
Wants to finish his website before focusing on other things like learning ad monetization

entities: Jake's (restaurant, likely 'Jake and Telly's' or similar nearby)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was too lazy to do a vlog yesterday because I was working until 2 AM and then got really tired and went to sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on attaching or creating files to download for a Q&A.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to make two tables, one for race distances and another for circles, because combining them didn't fit right.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm creating files for the circles, but it's more intricate than I planned, so it's taking longer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My brother is helping with my motorbike, and my mom is handling selling my car, so I don't have to do anything.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: I'm trying to figure out why my website isn't making money, even though I've only made $230 total from it.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: My website is loaded with ads, and I'm listening to ad tutorials while working on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm reading about experiments and guides to monetize my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to watch videos and work on my website while riding my bike indoors.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had lunch with my mom at Jake's Tweeter yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I finished a page on my website and showed my mom the tables I was working on at the restaurant.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: I noticed my website looks weird on mobile, with inconsistent text sizes and table widths.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I called Adobe for help with mobile design, but they don't have programs in Creative Cloud to fix it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I suspect libertarians are all paid shills and plan to study them to prove it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I watched a video about libertarians for two and a half hours and took notes, but lost them accidentally.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to target libertarians because they're funded by billionaires pushing misleading ideas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe diverting resources to bicycles for everyone could be a better system.
